summary: The Chi-square test assesses associations between categorical variables by analyzing qualitative rather than numerical/quantitative information; it does not apply to direct measurement analysis like age ranges but focuses instead on discrete categories such as gender classifications for instance. This makes options involving its use beyond these parameters incorrect according to standard statistical practice guidelines provided here. To be precise regarding this specific query posed initially concerning which statement accurately describes a characteristic feature of using chi-squared tests correctly within their intended scope—qualifying answers would point towards recognizing how they handle non-numeric classification tasks effectively without misapplying methods designed differently suited elsewhere statistically speaking. Agent2 concludes based solely upon understanding from retrieved literature emphasizing application strictly toward qualitative assessments through cross-tabulations etc., thus ruling out any confusion
